chain.thought:
Add necessary columns to the table.
The question asks about the number of games where Archibald scored a point this season. Each row represents a game, and we need to check if Archibald is listed as a scorer. We extract the value from column "aberdeen scorer(s)" and create a different column "scorer" for each row. The datatype is String.

Select relevant rows.
The question aims to find out how many games Archibald scored in during the season. We need to look at all the rows where "archibald" appears in the "aberdeen scorer(s)" column to count the number of games.

Filter out useless columns.
similar words of the question link to columns:
games -> match day
archibald score -> aberdeen scorer(s)
column value of the question link to columns:
archibald -> aberdeen scorer(s)
semantic sentence of the question link to columns:
score a point -> aberdeen scorer(s)

The question is asking to determine the number of games where Archibald scored at least one point. Since each row represents a match and the "aberdeen scorer(s)" column lists the players who scored in each match, we need to filter or group the data to find matches where "archibald" appears in the scorer list. We can use the f_group_column(aberdeen scorer(s)) operation to analyze how many times Archibald appears as a scorer across different matches.

Archibald scored a point in all three games listed in the table. He scored in Match Day 4 (along with MacLachlan), Match Day 11 (as the sole scorer), and Match Day 28 (along with Brewster). Therefore, Archibald scored a point in 3 games this season.

judge_thought:

Row 11: Archibald (scored in the game against Third Lanark)
